Title: Merchant Screening Standard Operating Procedure
Content:
Merchant Screening SOP

PEP (Politically Exposed Person) Screening is an essential aspect of Anti-Money Laundering and KYC (Know Your Customer) regulation. It involves validating a merchant’s identity to determine if they are classified as a PEP.

During account activation, Merchant Operations (MO) will perform screening of the directors, shareholders, and commissioners’ names of the prospect client against the PEP name list. If a name matches the list, further actions will be required.

ComplyAdvantage is a new screening tool used by MO, which replaces KYC6. This tool screens merchants and connected parties, such as Authorized Signatories, all Directors, and all Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBOs), against sanctions, PEP, and adverse media databases.

Screening is a crucial component of KYC/CDD (Customer Due Diligence) procedures to protect Xendit from sanction, regulatory, and reputational risks.

Screening Guidelines

ComplyAdvantage (CA) has a total of seven Production Environments. Each country has its environment, along with a regional environment for merchants.

Generally, MO needs to login to CA to review the screening results, which include:

  • Adding notes to each profile to determine if it is a true positive hit or not.

  • Assigning a status to each profile.

  • Once all profiles have been reviewed, changing the status of the case.

  • If there are hits that are uncertain or true positive hits, escalating the case to the relevant Money Laundering Reporting Office for each country.

The following names are assigned to their corresponding countries:

  • Philippines: @Guada

  • Indonesia: @Paramitha Tennes

  • Thailand: @Olivia Mokarat

  • Singapore: @delwyn

  • Malaysia: @hongjie

  • Vietnam: @hongjie for now.

Concepts Used in ComplyAdvantage

  • Customer: A person or company that has a direct relationship with the company.

  • Customer Screening: The process of checking the customer against a global database of sanctions, warnings, fitness and probity, watchlists, PEPs, and adverse media.

  • Customer Monitoring: Continuously checking a person or company for changes in potential risk.

  • Cases: Created when there is a potential match identified by MO, further analyzed to determine if it is a false positive or a true positive.

  • Alerts: Signals indicating that an alert is generated during screening, prompting MO to check the alert and make decisions based on the findings.

What Needs to Be Screened?

  • Name of Directors

  • Name of UBOs

  • Name of All Offices

  • Name of Shareholders

  • Name of Entity that is Onboarding

  • Name of Entities that are Shareholders

Screening Workflow

To perform screening on ComplyAdvantage, please follow the guidelines outlined below:

  1. Login to CA Dashboard: Each MO agent will have login access. Enter "xendit" as the company name, your email address, and your login credentials, then click continue to access the Cases list page.

  2. Create a Search: In the CA dashboard, select Customer, then Create, and Create Person. Fill in the details including complete name, gender, date of birth (DOB), country of birth, and nationality. For Customer Identifier, input the merchant's BID number along with the merchant's details.

  3. Create a Case: A case is created if the profile has a hit. MO must determine whether the hit is a true or false positive. Assign a status from the dropdown based on the assessment.

  4. Escalate if Necessary: If there are hits and MO is uncertain or if true positive hits are found, escalate the case to the relevant Money Laundering Reporting Officer for each country.

Decision-Making by L2

Once MO creates a case and escalates it to the Reporting Officer (L2), they will receive notifications via the CA dashboard and on Slack. L2 will review the case profile and make one of the following decisions:

  • No Risk - Accept: MO agent can onboard the merchant with confirmation from Compliance.

  • Manageable Risk - Accept: MO agent can onboard the merchant with conditions monitored by Compliance.

  • Unacceptable Risk - Reject: MO agent must reject the merchant's application.

MO must check the case status on the CA dashboard and use filters to narrow search results as needed.

FAQs

What is PEP Screening?

PEP (Politically Exposed Person) Screening is an essential part of Anti-Money Laundering and KYC regulations that involves validating a merchant’s identity to determine if they are classified as a PEP.

What does ComplyAdvantage do?

ComplyAdvantage is a screening tool used by Merchant Operations to screen merchants and connected parties against sanctions, PEP, and adverse media databases.

What steps are involved in the screening workflow?

The screening workflow includes logging into the CA dashboard, creating a search, creating a case, and escalating if necessary.
Description: Learn the essential procedures and guidelines for merchant screening, including PEP screening and the use of ComplyAdvantage.
